title: Asthma Care Quick Reference
description: A comprehensive guide on asthma management, including medication information, patient education, and resources.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: This document provides essential information on asthma management, focusing on inhaled corticosteroids and long-acting beta-agonists.

subsections.title: Inhaled Corticosteroids (ICS)
subsections.content: ICSs are the most effective medications for long-term control of persistent asthma. They are inhaled directly to the lungs to reduce inflammation.

subsections.title: Long-Acting Beta2-Agonists (LABAs)
subsections.content: LABAs should be used in combination with ICSs for asthma control. They are not for quick relief and have specific usage guidelines.

subsections.title: Common Questions
subsections.content: Addressing patient concerns about ICSs, including side effects, growth effects, and safety during pregnancy.

subsections.title: Response to LABA Questions
subsections.content: Clarifying the benefits and risks of LABAs, including the FDA warning and proper usage.

sections.title: Resources
sections.content: A list of educational resources and organizations for asthma management.

keyPoints: ICSs are first-line for persistent asthma control.
LABAs must be used with ICSs and not for acute symptoms.
Monitor for side effects and adjust doses as needed.
